label: About
title: About The Inlay Foundry of Hidden Letterforms
short_blurb: Built from 1124 notes from the last 30 days, 10 inspected links, and 9 unique source objects.
body: For the visual seed, the process used @1yamada: emily kraus (pbs.twimg.com), then translated that source image into low raking workshop light from high clerestory windows, warm brass glints, cold blue fog trapped in glass vessels, soft shadows across scratched surfaces and materials like raw stained linen, blackened slate, lacquered wood, brass type, and rice paper. The source set was de-duplicated before the brief, then bound to @1yamada: emily kraus, @elder_plinius: breaking someone just dropped the most advanced steganography platform ever, and 50 Watts.

gpt-5.5 inspected the finished plate and mapped 10 visible objects, including large scarred linen wall with red loops and ink stains, dark incised slate wall panel on the right, suspended character-mark textile canopy, and central hanging paper folio carousel. The package, masks, interpretation, and enhancement plan were written from that run at tmp/daily-process-runs/daily-process-2026-04-26T00-57-14-203Z.

typography.body_family: 'DFE Source Serif 4', Iowan Old Style, Georgia, serif
typography.rationale: Fraunces gives the title an organic, seed-label quality; Source Serif keeps the explanation calm and readable.
